title: Agent Handoff with Context
constraints: The full conversation history must be preserved during handoff
Agent B must be aware of what Agent A discussed with the user
You may not ask the user to repeat information after a handoff
The handoff mechanism must be explicit, not implicit
hints: Pass the conversation history as part of the handoff payload to Agent B
In OpenAI Agents SDK, handoffs automatically carry context — make sure you're using them correctly
In LangGraph, store conversation messages in the state so they persist across node transitions

starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

sales_agent = Agent(
    name="Sales Agent",
    instructions="You are a sales agent. Help customers choose the right product plan.",
)

onboarding_agent = Agent(
    name="Onboarding Agent",
    instructions="You are an onboarding specialist. Help new customers set up their account.",
)

# BUG: When sales hands off to onboarding, conversation context is lost
# TODO: Preserve conversation history during the handoff

# Simulate a multi-turn conversation
result = Runner.run_sync(sales_agent, "I'm interested in the Pro plan for my team of 10")
print("Sales:", result.final_output)

# User is ready to onboard — but onboarding agent has no context
result = Runner.run_sync(onboarding_agent, "Great, I'd like to set up my account now")
print("Onboarding:", result.final_output)
starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from typing import TypedDict

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

class State(TypedDict):
    user_message: str
    sales_response: str
    onboarding_response: str

def sales_node(state: State) -> dict:
    prompt = f"You are a sales agent. The customer says: {state['user_message']}"
    response = llm.invoke(prompt)
    return {"sales_response": response.content}

def onboarding_node(state: State) -> dict:
    # BUG: No context from sales conversation is passed
    # TODO: Include the sales conversation in the prompt
    prompt = f"You are an onboarding specialist. The customer says: Set up my account now."
    response = llm.invoke(prompt)
    return {"onboarding_response": response.content}

graph = StateGraph(State)
graph.add_node("sales", sales_node)
graph.add_node("onboarding", onboarding_node)
graph.add_edge(START, "sales")
graph.add_edge("sales", "onboarding")
graph.add_edge("onboarding", END)

app = graph.compile()
result = app.invoke({"user_message": "I'm interested in the Pro plan for my team of 10", "sales_response": "", "onboarding_response": ""})
print("Onboarding:", result["onboarding_response"])
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

sales_agent = Agent(
    role="Sales Agent",
    goal="Help customers choose the right product plan",
    backstory="You are a sales agent.",
    llm=llm,
)

onboarding_agent = Agent(
    role="Onboarding Agent",
    goal="Help new customers set up their account",
    backstory="You are an onboarding specialist.",
    llm=llm,
)

sales_task = Task(
    description="Customer says: I'm interested in the Pro plan for my team of 10",
    expected_output="A sales response about the Pro plan",
    agent=sales_agent,
)

# BUG: Onboarding task has no context from the sales conversation
# TODO: Pass sales conversation context to the onboarding task
onboarding_task = Task(
    description="Help the customer set up their account",
    expected_output="Onboarding steps for the customer",
    agent=onboarding_agent,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[sales_agent, onboarding_agent], tasks=[sales_task, onboarding_task], process=Process.sequential)
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)

evaluationCriteria.label: Conversation context preserved
evaluationCriteria.description: The full conversation history from Agent A is available to Agent B after handoff

evaluationCriteria.label: No repeated questions
evaluationCriteria.description: Agent B does not ask the user to repeat information already provided to Agent A

evaluationCriteria.label: Seamless handoff experience
evaluationCriteria.description: The transition between agents feels continuous to the user

evaluationCriteria.label: Explicit handoff mechanism
evaluationCriteria.description: The handoff is done through an explicit mechanism, not accidental state sharing
